relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Jesus moves from Galilee toward Judea beyond the Jordan.
Crowds follow and healing continues.
The Pharisees test him with a divorce question.
Jesus returns to creation’s beginning.
Union is framed as leaving, joining, and becoming one flesh.
What the Divine joins is not to be treated lightly by human separation.
They appeal to Moses and release documents.
Jesus names hardness of heart as the concession, not the original design.
He protects covenant from casual abandonment.
The disciples feel the weight of the teaching.
Not every person receives the same word in the same way.
Different embodied callings exist, including lives set apart for heaven’s reign.
Children are brought for blessing, but the disciples rebuke them.
Jesus opens the way to children because heaven’s reign belongs to such as these.
He blesses them with his hands.
A young man asks what good thing leads to age-long life.
Jesus directs him toward the good and the commands.
The commands against harm are named.
Honor and neighbor-love complete the list.
The young man has guarded these but senses lack.
Wholeness asks him to release possessions, give to the poor, and follow.
He goes away grieving because wealth holds him.
Jesus names the difficulty of wealth entering heaven’s reign.
The camel and needle image exposes the impossibility of wealth as security.
The disciples are stunned and ask who can be saved.
What is impossible with humans is possible with the Divine.
Peter asks what follows for those who left everything.
Jesus speaks of renewal, thrones, and restored Israel.
Loss for the name is gathered into larger inheritance.
The order reverses: first last, last first.
